skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables
stem: In the equation above, p and q are positive constants. How many times does the graph of the equation above intersect the graph of the equation y = p + q in the xy-plane?
rationale: Choice A is correct. Since p and q are positive, the parabola y = px² − q opens upward and has its vertex on the y-axis at (0, −q), below the x-axis. The line y = p + q is horizontal, and because p and q are positive, p + q > 0, so the line lies above the x-axis. A horizontal line above the x-axis intersects an upward-opening parabola with vertex below the x-axis at exactly two points. Choice B is wrong because the line is above the vertex. Choice C is wrong because the graphs do intersect. Choice D is wrong because a line cannot intersect a parabola in more than two points.
